summary: Regional Fleet support + route reads through the Fleet API when no usable owner-api credential exists. Replaces the North-America-hardcoded fleetAPIAudience with a fleetAPIBase() resolver (TESLA_FLEET_API_URL env > persisted [fleet].api_base > NA default) used by fleet-register/login/partner-token, and persists api_base. newClient() points the read client at the regional Fleet API with the Fleet bearer when the owner-api token is absent or stale, with a Fleet-specific 401 auto-refresh (makeTeslaFleetRefreshCallback). In Fleet mode the client rewrites owner-api vehicle-data subset paths (data_request/{name}) to the Fleet vehicle_data?endpoints= form and unwraps the nested response back to the owner-api shape, so the get-data-* subset commands and reachability work on Fleet instead of 404ing.
reason: tesla-pp-cli was generated from owner-api traffic: reads use the top-level owner-api bearer and Fleet was wired for commands only, hardcoded to North America. 2021+ vehicles and non-NA accounts (e.g. EU) have no working owner-api read path, so products/ready/vehicle_data returned owner-api 404/401 even with a valid Fleet token, and partner registration hit the wrong region (HTTP 421/412).
validated_outcome: Unit tests (fleetAPIBase, teslaShouldUseFleetForReads, SaveFleetAPIBase, rewriteFleetSubsetPath, unwrapFleetSubset, AuthHeader fleet-bearer, no-persist regression, newClient routing) plus full module build/vet/test/govulncheck pass. Read-routing uses a non-persisted UseFleetBearer flag + a client-level base URL so a token refresh never writes a transient auth_header/base_url to disk. Validated live against an EU vehicle: products, full vehicle_data, ready --force-refresh, and get-data-* subset commands return real data with zero env vars; the fleet 401->refresh path was exercised with an invalid token and re-minted cleanly without polluting the config. Hardened after an independent Codex adversarial review: Fleet base-host allowlist (https + tesla.com, loopback for tests/relay) blocks bearer exfiltration via TESLA_FLEET_API_URL/api_base; TESLA_FLEET_TOKEN is honored for read routing; cacheKey resolves AuthHeader before reading AuthSource; the refresh callback validates client_id. Fleet drive_state GPS is gated on the vehicle_location scope (decoded from the Fleet JWT): with the scope, drive_state also requests the location_data endpoint and merges GPS into the response; without it, drive_state is fetched alone so the call never 403s.
